slides.title: Origami Vocabulary
slides.content:
Origami is the Japanese art of folding paper. Let's learn the key words:

📄 fold — to bend paper to make a shape
🤲 crumple — to squash paper into a ball
📐 edge — the side or border of the paper
📌 corner — the point where two edges meet
slides.tip: Fold = doblar. Be careful: fold neatly, don't crumple!

slides.title: Art Materials
slides.content:
Things we use to make art:

🖍️ markers — coloured pens with thick tips
🎨 watercolours — paints you mix with water
🖍️ chalk — soft sticks for drawing on a board or paper
✂️ scissors — a tool for cutting paper
🧴 glue — sticky liquid to join things together
slides.tip: Scissors is always plural in English! We say 'a pair of scissors'.

slides.title: Origami Day
slides.html:
Today is Origami Day in class! Miss Green gives everyone a square piece of paper.

“First, fold the paper in half,” she says. “Match the edges carefully. Now fold one corner down.”

Tom crumples his paper by accident! “Oh no!” he says. Miss Green gives him a new one.

At the end, everyone has a beautiful paper bird. They colour them with markers and watercolours. Some children use chalk too. They cut out extra wings with scissors and stick them on with glue.

slides.title: Do I need...?
slides.content:
We use Do I need...? to ask if something is necessary:

❓ Do I need scissors?
✅ Yes, you do.
❌ No, you don't.

We use You don't need... to say something is NOT necessary:

🔹 You don't need glue for this project.
🔹 You don't need watercolours today.
slides.tip: Do I need = Do I have to have it? You don't need = You can do it without it!

slides.title: What Do I Need?
slides.problem:
Answer the questions about making an origami bird:

1. Do I need paper? _____
2. Do I need scissors? _____
3. Do I need glue? _____
4. Do I need watercolours? _____
steps: 1. You always need paper for origami --> Yes, you do.
2. Origami is only folding, no cutting --> No, you don't.
3. Origami is only folding, no sticking --> No, you don't.
4. You can colour it after, but you don't have to --> No, you don't.
slides.answer: 1. Yes, you do.  2. No, you don't.  3. No, you don't.  4. No, you don't.

slides.title: Commas in Lists
slides.content:
When we write a list of three or more things, we use commas to separate them:

✅ I need paper, scissors, and glue.
✅ She has markers, chalk, and watercolours.

Rules:

🔹 Put a comma after each item
🔹 Use and before the last item
🔹 No comma needed for only TWO items: paper and scissors
slides.tip: Commas in a list are like little pauses when you read. They help you separate each item!

slides.title: Add the Commas
slides.problem:
Add commas to these sentences:

1. I need paper scissors and glue.
2. She likes red blue and green.
3. We fold crumple and cut the paper.
4. Tom has markers chalk and watercolours.
steps: 1. Three items: paper + scissors + glue --> I need paper, scissors, and glue.
2. Three items: red + blue + green --> She likes red, blue, and green.
3. Three items: fold + crumple + cut --> We fold, crumple, and cut the paper.
4. Three items: markers + chalk + watercolours --> Tom has markers, chalk, and watercolours.
slides.answer: 1. paper, scissors, and glue  2. red, blue, and green  3. fold, crumple, and cut  4. markers, chalk, and watercolours

slides.title: The Art Cupboard
slides.html:
“Do I need any markers?” asks Lily.

“You don't need markers today,” says Miss Green. “You need paper, scissors, and glue.”

Lily goes to the art cupboard. She finds chalk, watercolours, and scissors, but there isn't any glue!

“Miss Green, do I need glue?” she asks. “You don't need glue for the first step. First, fold the paper. Match the edges and corners. Don't crumple it!”

slides.title: Origami Instructions
slides.content:
When we give instructions for origami, we use action words:

1️⃣ Fold the paper in half.
2️⃣ Match the edges.
3️⃣ Press the corners down.
4️⃣ Don't crumple the paper!

We can also say what materials we need:
You need paper, scissors, and markers.
You don't need glue or chalk.

slides.title: Origami Challenge
slides.problem:
Read the instructions and answer:

To make an origami boat, you need paper. Fold the paper in half. Fold the corners to the middle edge. You don't need scissors or glue.

1. Do I need scissors?
2. What do I fold first?
3. Do I need glue?
steps: 1. The instructions say you don't need scissors --> No, you don't.
2. The first step says fold the paper in half --> The paper in half.
3. The instructions say you don't need glue --> No, you don't.
slides.answer: 1. No, you don't.  2. The paper in half.  3. No, you don't.

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🦢 Origami words: fold, crumple, edge, corner
🎨 Art materials: markers, watercolours, chalk, scissors, glue
❓ Do I need...? --> Yes, you do. / No, you don't.
🚫 You don't need... = it is not necessary
✏️ Commas in lists: item1, item2, and item3
📝 Only use commas for THREE or more items!
